title: ficus copiosa var. muriculata
intro: Ficus copiosa var. muriculata, also known as the bengkong fig or bengkung fig, is a plant species within the ficus genus, native to Southeast Asia. The medicinal properties of Ficus copiosa var. muriculata include its high concentration of saponins and flavonoids, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits associated with this plant include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory issues. Ficus copiosa var. muriculata is commonly prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions, which are consumed orally to harness its medicinal properties.
